/QuickLinks.aspx
The Cumberland County Bureau of Elections is responsible for running a fair, safe, accurate and secure election for our voters and residents. The office is committed to providing clear, transparent, and reliable voter services to the community.
Responsibilities
Maintain voter registration records
Conduct elections
Prepare official ballots
Train polling staff
Board of Elections Meetings
FAQs
Where do I vote?
After your Voter Registration Application has been processed, you will receive a Certificate of Voter Registration that will indicate your polling place and address. You can also contact the County Election Office for your polling place location or check our
online listing of polling places
.
How do I check the status of my Absentee/Mail-In Application or Ballot?
Click here to access
Mail-In/Absentee Application or Ballot Tracking
.
How do I cancel my annual/permanent Mail-In or Absentee Ballot request?
Please complete the
Annual/Permanent Status Cancellation Form
. This form can be returned to our office by regular mail, email to
bureauofelections@cumberlandcountypa.gov
or by fax to 717-240-7759.
What are the voter identification requirements?
For current information about the Voter ID requirements, see our
Voter Identification page
.
What is the deadline to apply for an absentee or mail-in ballot?
Absentee & mail-in ballot applications must be received by the County Elections Office no later than  on the Tuesday prior to the primary or election. However, we highly recommend that you apply as early as possible to allow time for necessary mailings. Postmarks are not accepted.
Election Dates & Deadlines
When must the voted ballot be returned?
All voted absentee & mail-in ballots must be received by the County Election Office no later than  on the day of the primary or election.
Are Voter Registration records available to the public?
All Voter Registration records are considered public documents and are available for public inspection under the supervision of the County Election office staff.
How do I become a candidate?
Anyone wishing to become a candidate for a county, school or municipal level elected office may pick up a petition, instructions, and an election calendar from our office or may print these items from the "
Running for Office
" section of our website.
View All FAQs
/FAQ.aspx
